data.title: Key facts: HDFC Bank Drives Nifty Up 321 Points; Record Nifty Bank High
data.description:
• On November 26, 2025, HDFC Bank boosted the Nifty, which rose 321 points to 26,205, while the Nifty Bank index hit a record high of 59,528, up 708 points.1
  • On September 24, 2025, HDFC Bank executed multiple block deals, trading 292,136 shares at 1002.10 INR and 1,976,024 shares at 1007.75 INR, reflecting substantial trading activity.2

data.title: Key facts: HCL Technologies partners with SAP on Physical AI; shares rise 1.03%
data.description:
• HCL Technologies Ltd (HCLTECH) has entered into a collaboration with SAP to advance initiatives centered around Physical AI.1
  • HCL Technologies experienced a gain of 1.03 percent, reaching Rs 1,617.20, amid a broader rise in IT stocks influenced by expectations of a US Federal Reserve rate cut and a weakening Indian rupee.2

data.title: Key facts: Bajaj Finance shines in NBFC sector; shares rise 31% to 43%
data.description:
• Market expert Deven Choksey identified Bajaj Finance as a top performer in the NBFC sector, citing its rapid loan disbursement, effective capital management, and strong asset quality.1
  • Bajaj Finance closed up 1.24% at 26,205.30, contributing to the Nifty index's gains, with shares rising 31% to 43% since the peak on September 27, 2024.23

data.title: Key facts: Axis Bank issues Rs. 5,000 Crore debentures; Nifty rises 321 points; Anand Viswanathan named Chief Risk Officer
data.description:
• On November 26, 2025, Axis Bank issued 500,000 long-term debentures worth Rs. 5,000 Crore, with a 7.27% annual coupon rate, rated 'CRISIL AAA/Stable' and others.1
  • On November 26, 2025, Axis Bank boosted the Nifty, which rose by 321 points to 26,205, while the Nifty Bank index hit a record high of 59,528, up 708 points.2
  • Axis Bank Limited has appointed Anand Viswanathan as the new Chief Risk Officer, effective January 1, 2026, succeeding Amit Talgeri, who will conclude his second term on December 31, 2025.3

data.title: Key facts: Adani Enterprises rights issue dates set; IHC unit sells shares
data.description:
• Adani Enterprises Ltd (ADANIENT) is offering a rights issue. Key dates: rights entitlements credited on November 18, renunciation ends December 5, issue closes December 10, allotment on December 11.1
  • A unit of International Holding Company (IHC) executed a bulk deal, selling 842,924 shares of Adani Enterprises Ltd, as reported by the National Stock Exchange (NSE).2

data.title: Key facts: WIPRO LTD partners for AI research; faces performance challenges
data.description:
• WIPRO LTD has entered into a partnership to establish a joint research program focused on artificial intelligence (AI).1
  • Wipro Technologies has underperformed in the past year, facing challenges from AI disruption impacting routine jobs like call centers and admin support.2

data.title: Key facts: TCS to drive Aldi South's digital shift; partners with TPG on AI
data.description:
• Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) will lead digital transformation for Aldi South, enhancing its digital capabilities globally.1
  • Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) has entered into a partnership with private equity firm TPG, committing $2 billion in equity to establish AI data centres.2
  • On November 21, 2025, Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) shares rose 1.35% to Rs 3,162, driven by a potential US rate cut and a weaker rupee.34

data.title: Key facts: Infosys shares rise 1.93%; underperformance due to AI threats
data.description:
• Infosys shares rose 1.93% to Rs 1,559.55, boosting the Nifty IT index by 1.48%, driven by expectations of a US Federal Reserve rate cut and a weaker Indian rupee.1
  • Infosys has underperformed over the past year due to rising AI threats in the service sector, impacting routine jobs in call centers and admin support.2

data.title: Key facts: Indian Continent Investment sells 0.6% stake in Bharti Airtel; Strong ARPU growth expected; Bharti Telecom to raise $954 million
data.description:
• On November 26, 2025, Indian Continent Investment sold a 0.6% stake in Bharti Airtel, impacting the stock price, which closed at Rs 2,126.8 on the NSE.12
  • Bharti Airtel shows strong ARPU compared to Jio and improving operations in Africa. A potential ARPU of ₹300 is priced in, with a possible 15–20% increase aligned with market trends.3
  • Bharti Telecom plans to raise about $954 million by issuing short-term bonds with maturities of two and three years, offering annual coupons of 7.30% and 7.40%.4

data.title: Palm Oil Rises Further
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures rose for a second session on Thursday, hovering around MYR 4,060 per tonne amid strength in Dalian edible oils and extended bargain hunting.

Prices recovered further from a five-month low hit earlier this week, on expectations that India, the world’s largest buyer, may lift imports by about 20% in the new marketing year starting this November, supported by more competitive pricing.

In top supplier Indonesia, authorities have tightened oversight of the palm oil sector following suspected export data manipulation, a move that could slightly curb near-term shipments and provide limited price support.

So far this week, the contract is set for a second straight weekly decline, down around 0.2%, as supply-side pressures outweigh gains.

Cargo surveyors estimated Malaysia’s exports fell between 16.4%–18.8% in the first 25 days of November from the prior month, while October output surged 11% to its highest since August 2015, pushing inventories to a 6-1/2-year peak.

data.title: Train runs down, kills 11 in China's worst rail accident in over a decade
data.description:
A train ran into a group of railway workers on Thursday, killing 11 and injuring two, officials in China's southwestern city of Kunming said, the deadliest rail accident in more than a decade.

The train, testing earthquake detection equipment, hit workers on a curved section of track at the city's Luoyang Town railway station, the officials said.

The station in Yunnan province has resumed normal services, and the cause of the accident is being investigated, they added in a statement.

China's rail network is the world's largest, spanning more than 160,000 km (100,000 miles) and racking up billions of trips each year.

Though praised for its efficiency, it has drawn scrutiny after several high-profile incidents, such as a 2011 crash in the eastern province of Zhejiang that killed 40 and injured 200.

Nine were killed in 2021 when a train in the northwestern province of Gansu ran into workers on a section of the Lanzhou-Xinjiang railway.

data.title: India's Patel Engineering jumps on getting orders worth 7.98 bln rupees
data.description:
** Shares of Patel Engineering jump 11.1% to 36.86 rupees

** Co gets orders worth 7.98 bln rupees ($89.4 mln) from Hyderabad-based Saidax Engineers and Infrastructure

** Receives two Letter of Intents for excavation and transportation of coal at a project in central state of Chhattisgarh, for South Eastern Coalfields Ltd

** 29.5 mln shares traded so far vs 30-day avg of 1.9 mln shares

** Stock down 29.07% YTD

Bank of Singapore to invest in hiring, tech to boost assets, CEO says
data.description:
Key points:
  • AUM up nearly 20% to exceed $145 billion in Q3
  • Hiring to accelerate again in 2026, CEO says
  • Tech, ultra-rich focus key to growth strategy

By Yantoultra Ngui

Bank of Singapore plans more investment in hiring and technology as it aims to climb into Asia's top five private banks within five years after assets under management rose nearly 20% to exceed $145 billion in the third quarter, Chief Executive Jason Moo said.

Asia's wealth surge anchors the push. Global high-net-worth individual wealth rose 4.2% in 2024, while Asia-Pacific's climbed 4.8%, second only to North America, according to Capgemini's world wealth report.

The region's high-net-worth individual population grew 2.7%, expanding the client base for private banks.

High-net-worth individuals refers to those with at least $1 million in investable assets, while ultra-high-net-worth people are those with $30 million or more, according to Capgemini.

"We hope to be within the top five in the next three to five years," Moo told Reuters in an interview. Finews, which tracks standalone private banks, currently ranks Bank of Singapore, the private banking arm of Oversea-Chinese Banking Corp , seventh in the region.

Bank of Singapore's AUM stood at about $120 billion when Moo took over in early 2023. It has since climbed to more than $145 billion in the third quarter despite the bank raising its minimum account size to $5 million from $3 million last year.

The number of relationship managers has reached 500, from roughly 400 in 2023, and Moo said hiring will accelerate again in 2026.

"We hired aggressively in 2024, we hired moderately in 2025. We'll probably switch back to the aggressive mode in 2026. So we'll continue that trend, continue that runway for the next few years," he said.

Bank of Singapore also plans more bespoke products for clients with $100 million or more in assets, according to Moo.

Ultra-high-net-worth assets grew close to 20% in the first three quarters this year, while assets from financial intermediaries such as external asset managers rose over 30%.

Moo said the bank is also investing in proprietary asset allocation technology to incorporate local currencies and insurance holdings into portfolio planning, aiming to make wealth management more tailored for clients.

Hong Kong, its largest office outside Singapore, has already met its target of growing AUM by 50% from 2024 to 2026, more than a year ahead of schedule, according to Moo.

Dubai remains a priority, with the bank ranking third among private banks there behind Julius Baer and UBS and on track to account for 20% of AUM by 2027, Moo said.

He added that a booking centre in Dubai is under consideration if client demand rises. Singapore and Hong Kong are its current booking centres.

Looking ahead, Moo said the bank is exploring ways to leverage OCBC's regional presence to build an integrated onshore-offshore private banking network across the region.

OCBC is Southeast Asia's second-largest bank by assets, with key markets including Greater China, Malaysia and Indonesia. OCBC also owns an 88.2% stake in insurer Great Eastern Holdings , LSEG data showed.

data.title: Vanke's Bond Payment Delay Proposal Sparks Selloff in Chinese Property Developers
data.description:
By Jiahui Huang

China Vanke's proposal to delay repayment of an onshore bond led to trading halts in three other local notes and triggered a selloff in Chinese property developers, ratcheting up fears about the country's drawn-out real-estate crisis.

Vanke, one of China's biggest real-estate companies, was once regarded as among the country's most solid developers. It is among the few Chinese major developers that has yet to default amid the country's massive property bust.

In a filing late Wednesday, the company said it is seeking to delay payment of the principal on a 2 billion yuan ($282.5 million) medium-term note due Dec. 15.

The move sparked a broad selloff in the bond and equity markets. Some of Vanke's onshore bonds dropped by more than 30%, triggering trading halts in Shenzhen. Chinese real-estate stocks fell sharply in Hong Kong, with the Hang Seng Mainland Properties Index, a gauge of developers listed in the city, dropping as much as 2.1% in early trading Thursday.

Shares of China Vanke slid as much as 8.5% in Hong Kong, hitting a record low before paring the losses to 4.4% by midday. Shimao Group led the decline among other property stocks, dropping 4.8%. Longfor Group fell 2.1% and Agile Group shed 1.3%. The benchmark Hang Seng Index rose 0.3%.

Vanke's surprise request to delay repayment on local debt highlights the continuing cash-flow uncertainties facing Chinese developers and has spurred more questions about the continued spread of the real-estate downturn. China's property sector, once a driver of the country's economic growth, has been in a yearslong slump, with many major property developers suffering liquidity crises and defaulting on their debts.

"Vanke's proposed delay in repayment reflects the firm's lingering liquidity crunch, which weighs heavily on its debt-servicing capabilities," Morningstar analyst Jeff Zhang said.

The developer, a top property pick among many analysts, been getting support from state-owned Shenzhen Metro Group amid the sector crisis. About one-third of Vanke's shares are held by the rail operator. In early November, Vanke said it could borrow up to 22 billion yuan from Shenzhen Metro before June 30, 2026, with 2.29 billion yuan still available for withdrawal.

The lynchpin is the negotiation with bondholders, who will likely require higher interest rates and additional pledges for the extension, Zhang said.

"If the negotiation falls through, we foresee significant deterioration in investors' sentiment on China property sector, as Vanke is expected to remain safe from defaults," Zhang said.

That said, Zhang thinks China's central and local governments would aim to contain the spillover effects of Vanke's troubles, so they may intervene by facilitating the agreement with major creditors.

Earlier this month, S&P Global Ratings downgraded China Vanke's credit rating further into junk territory, to CCC from B-minus, saying the company's financial commitments appear to be unsustainable.

The ratings company in its report said Vanke's contracted sales between January and October have shrunk by about 43% from a year earlier, weaker than its previous expectation of a 40% drop for the full year. In 2026, it estimated that the company's contracted sales could slip further to 103 billion yuan.

"China Vanke could default on its debt obligations if it does not receive timely and sufficient loans from its largest shareholder, Shenzhen Metro," S&P Global Ratings said.

Write to Jiahui Huang at jiahui.huang@wsj.com

data.title: Italy - Factors to watch on November 27
data.description:
The following factors could affect Italian markets on Thursday.

Reuters has not verified the newspaper reports, and cannot vouch for their accuracy. New items are marked with (*).

For a complete list of diary events in Italy please click on .

POLITICS

Italy's Senate has postponed debate on a landmark bill that would stipulate for the first time that sex without consent constitutes rape, exposing tensions within Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni's coalition.

BANKS

Italy is considering an additional increase in the IRAP corporate tax applied to domestic banks to help finance costly amendments to next year’s budget, political sources said.

The draft budget already raises the IRAP rate for banks and insurance companies by two percentage points. Rome is now evaluating a further 0.5-percentage-point hike, but only for major lenders, the sources added.

ECONOMY

ISTAT releases November consumer and business confidence data (0900 GMT) and September industry sales data (1000 GMT).

DEBT

Treasury sells 2.25-2.75 billion euros 2.85% BTP bonds due Feb. 1, 2031; 2.25-2.75 billion euros 3.45% BTP bonds due Feb. 1, 2036; 3.5-4.0 billion euros new CCTeu bonds due April 15, 2035. Subscriptions close at 1000 GMT.

COMPANIES

STELLANTIS

Chinese battery maker CATL started building Spain's largest battery factory on Wednesday, a 4.1-billion-euro project with Stellantis that highlights Europe's reliance on Chinese technology even as Brussels seeks to tighten trade rules.

MFE-MEDIAFOREUROPE

The TV group controlled by Italy's Berlusconi family said on Wednesday it had agreed to buy a 32.9% stake in Portuguese media company Impresa , noting that the transaction would have no impact on 2025 financial year forecasts.

ZEST

Capital increase ends.

DIARY

Pope Leo starts visit to Turkey; ends on November 30.

Rome, association for the economic development of southern Italy (SVIMEZ) presents report (0815 GMT); EU Commission Executive Vice President Raffaele Fitto delivers opening address by video message; expected attendees include European Affairs and National Recovery and Resilience Plan (NRRP) Minister Tommaso Foti, Parliamentary Budget Office (UPB) President Lilia Cavallari.

Rome, UniCredit CEO Andrea Orcel addresses Parliamentary Committee on Italian bank mergers (1030 GMT).

Rome, Leonardo holds event "Michelangelo Project, the security dome" (1600 GMT).

Rome, annual conference on Italy's birth crisis "Stati Generali della Natalità" starts (0800 GMT); ends on November 28. On Thursday expected attendees include ISTAT President Francesco Maria Chelli, welfare institute INPS President Gabriele Fava, social security agency INAIL Director Marcello Fiori, Brunello Cucinelli Chairman Brunello Cucinelli.

((Milan newsroom, e-mail: reutersitaly@thomsonreuters.com))
